other_observers: Mark Peterson, Joey Kellner, Lisa Edwards, and about twenty others.; I believe that the original finder was Dave Leatherman, and that the bird was identified by Joe Mammoser.
description: The bird was generally light below and blue-gray above. It had a blue-gray head with a black area around the eye, and white wing bars. The throat and belly were yellow, with a diffuse orange patch in the breast area.Behaviors: The bird was high in the treetops the entire time we were observing it. It flew from tree to tree several times, always staying near the tops of the trees.Call: A steady flat buzzing. My hearing is not the best, and others no doubt heard it much better.Plumage: Alternate
